title: NC House Speaker Tim Moore Pulls Tricks to Avoid Going on Record Against Abolition
author: James Silberman
orientation: Free the States reports that NC House Speaker Tim Moore twice rerouted HB158 (the Constitutional Amendment/Life at Fertilization bill) to a different committee in order to void Rep. Larry Pittman's discharge petitions, shielding pro-life Republican legislators from having to vote on record for or against abolishing abortion. The piece frames these procedural maneuvers as cowardly tricks protecting the political careers of an establishment that opposes immediate abolition.

questions.question: What specific procedural maneuver did Speaker Tim Moore use to block HB158, and how many times did he use it?
questions.answer: Twice, Moore reassigned HB158 to a new committee immediately after Rep. Larry Pittman filed a discharge petition, which voided each petition. The first move sent the bill from House Judiciary 1 to the Families, Children, and Aging Policy Committee on July 13; the second move (within minutes of Pittman's August 4 petition) sent it on to House Judiciary 2.
questions.quote: For the second time, North Carolina House Speaker Tim Moore has moved the bill to abolish abortion in North Carolina (NC HB158) to a different committee in response to a discharge petition being filed by the bill's author, Representative Larry Pittman.

questions.question: What is a discharge petition and what does it require to succeed?
questions.answer: It is a motion to pull a bill out of committee and bring it directly to the chamber floor. If a majority of the chamber's members sign the petition, the bill becomes eligible for a floor vote.
questions.quote: A discharge petition is a motion to bring a bill out of committee and onto the floor of the chamber. If a majority of the body signs the petition, the bill is then eligible to be heard on the floor.

questions.question: How quickly did Moore reroute the bill after Pittman's second discharge petition was announced?
questions.answer: Less than three minutes. Moore directed the clerk to remove HB158 from the Families committee and refer it to Judiciary 2 almost immediately after Pittman finished giving notice of his petition.
questions.quote: This time, Moore wasted hardly three minutes before moving the bill again, this time to House Judiciary 2 Committee.

questions.question: What does Free the States argue is the real motive behind the NC pro-life Republican establishment's behavior?
questions.answer: The article argues the establishment opposes HB158 but desperately wants to avoid being recorded voting against the abolition of abortion, because a public vote would expose their opposition to immediate abolition and damage their political careers.
questions.quote: The North Carolina pro-life Republican establishment not only opposes HB158 but desperately does not want to be forced to go on record. Tim Moore doesn't want a light to be shined on the darkness of his fellow pro-life Republican establishment representatives who oppose immediately abolishing abortion. So he plays tricks like these to protect the political careers of cowards.

questions.question: What rule did Pittman cite when announcing his August 4 discharge petition, and what did he include with it?
questions.answer: He invoked House Rule 39, gave notice that he would file the motion and petition with the principal clerk, and obtained a fiscal note to be included with the petition along with his written objections to that note.
questions.quote: Thank you Mr. Speaker, pursuant to House Rule 39, I am giving notice that I will file a motion and the requisite petition to discharge House Bill 158 Constitutional Amendment/Life at Fertilization from the Committee on Families, Children, and Aging Policy with the principal clerk today — which, of course, can be signed, if you're interested in signing, in the clerk's office. In accordance with the rules, I have obtained a fiscal note to be included with the petition, with my objections to the note reduced to writing.

questions.question: What concrete actions does the article ask supporters to take to counter Moore's tactics?
questions.answer: Share the article, and follow and financially support Free the States and Abolish Abortion North Carolina by donating, signing the AANC petition, subscribing to email and social media channels, and amplifying the message so Moore cannot continue these tricks unexposed.
questions.quote: Anti-abolitionists like Tim Moore will be able to keep playing his dirty tricks for as long as he can without being exposed for them.

questions.question: How does Pittman frame the abolition fight in religious terms during his floor announcement?
questions.answer: He closes his procedural notice with an explicit Trinitarian invocation, signaling that he treats the bill to recognize life at fertilization as a matter of obedience to God rather than mere politics.
questions.quote: In the name of the father, and of the son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.

related.title: Rep. Alan Seabaugh and the Louisiana House Ensure Murder by Abortion Continues
related.reason: Another case study of pro-life Republican legislators using procedural tactics (a gutting amendment) to kill an abolition bill while avoiding a clean up-or-down vote, mirroring Moore's behavior.

related.title: "I am a pro-life Oklahoman who voted to table a bill that would abolish all abortion"
related.reason: Documents the same pattern of pro-life establishment Republicans (here, OK Sen. Julie Daniels) using procedural tools like tabling to kill abolition bills.

related.title: Abolitionist, Not Pro-Life
related.reason: Provides the ideological framework behind the article's central distinction between the pro-life establishment Moore protects and the abolitionist movement pushing HB158.

related.title: Reps. Curt Nisly & John Jacob Author the Bill to Abolish Abortion in Indiana
related.reason: Companion profile of legislators (like Pittman in NC) who author state-level bills to immediately abolish abortion, useful for comparing how different state houses handle these bills.

related.title: State Rep. Bryan Slaton Files Bill to Immediately Abolish Abortion in Texas
related.reason: Another contemporary state-level abolition bill effort, providing context for the cross-state strategy Pittman is participating in.

related.title: Bill to Abolish Abortion in South Carolina Filed by State Rep. Jonathan Hill
related.reason: Neighboring state's parallel abolition bill effort, helping readers see the regional pattern of Southern state abolition bills and their treatment by GOP leadership.

related.title: Abortion Abolition Bill Introduced in Washington
related.reason: Demonstrates the national scope of the state abolition bill movement that HB158 belongs to, beyond the Republican-dominated South.
